summary: Declares unused dependency 'node-core-libs' resembling a core-libraries shim
details: node-fetch-utils@1.3.0's own code is a benign wrapper around node-fetch (lib/*.js and index.js only require 'node-fetch'). However, package.json declares an additional dependency 'node-core-libs@^1.0.0' that is never imported anywhere in the package. The unused name resembles a Node.js core-libraries shim and would be silently pulled into any installer's dependency tree on `npm install`. The risk is not in node-fetch-utils itself but in whatever tarball resolves for 'node-core-libs' — a pattern consistent with dependency-confusion / namespace-abuse where the lure package is benign and the harm is delivered transitively. The 'node-core-libs' package needs to be analyzed independently before this disposition can be finalized.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
